expected_observations.disease_decreases: Disease drops from prior 0.10 to ~0.05 because spots=no is strong evidence against disease (P(spots=yes|disease)=0.80). Even though disease explains wilting, the absence of spots outweighs it.
expected_observations.nutrient_deficiency_moderate: Nutrient deficiency rises from prior 0.20 to ~0.42. It explains yellow_leaves (P=0.70-0.80) and slow_growth (P=0.60-0.75), but wilting is better explained by overwatering alone.
expected_observations.overwatering_dominant: Overwatering rises from prior 0.25 to ~0.90, the strongest shift in the network. It directly explains wilting=yes (P=0.75 given overwatering, vs P=0.05 without) and contributes to yellow_leaves and slow_growth.
expected_observations.pest_decreases: Pest infestation drops from prior 0.15 to ~0.06 because spots=no argues against pests (P(spots=yes|pest)=0.65). Pests do not explain any of the observed symptoms well.
expected_observations.sunlight_moderate: Sunlight issue rises from prior 0.20 to ~0.38, contributing to yellow_leaves and slow_growth alongside nutrient deficiency and overwatering.

notes.description: Golden expected output for the Plant Doctor Bayesian inference scenario. Posteriors computed via exact variable elimination over the 9-variable BIF model given evidence yellow_leaves=yes, wilting=yes, spots=no, slow_growth=yes. Overwatering is the dominant root cause, with nutrient deficiency as a secondary contributor.

gibbs_sampling.note: Stochastic MCMC sampling. Overwatering is strongly indicated, so samples should concentrate there. With 10000 samples, posteriors should be within 0.05 of exact values.

junction_tree.note: Should match VE posteriors exactly (both are exact inference methods)

loopy_bp.note: Approximate message-passing. The overwatering signal is very strong, so convergence should be reliable. Nutrient deficiency and sunlight_issue have moderate posteriors that may show slightly more variance.

variable_elimination.note: Exact posteriors for all unobserved variables. Overwatering is the dominant diagnosis at ~0.90 because it directly explains both wilting (P=0.75) and contributes to yellow_leaves and slow_growth. Disease drops from 0.10 to ~0.05 because spots=no argues strongly against it.
